Part B.key_evidence: The central source is point-like and bright in the Science image, absent at that exact position in the Template, and appears as a compact positive residual in the Difference image. ndethist is only 1 with very large ncovhist=1853, so this is a first linked detection despite extensive prior coverage. The nearest PS1 source is far away (distpsnr1=17.8 arcsec), so the PS1 neighbor is likely unrelated.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: This is most consistent with a real solar-system object (Asteroid benchmark class). The required Science-vs-Template check for very low ndethist shows a source present in Science but not in the centered Template position, supporting motion rather than repeated variability at a fixed astrophysical location. The Difference image shows a clean positive point-like residual rather than a host-centered galaxy transient. The lack of a close cataloged counterpart and deltajd=0 also fit a moving object seen only at this epoch.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A possible alternative is a genuine astrophysical transient such as a supernova on a very faint host, since the residual is compact and positive. However, the single detection, no obvious host in the Template, and the need to explain why nothing persistent is centered there make the moving-object interpretation stronger than a fixed-position transient or variable star/AGN.
